title: The Brew Shop - Weekly Email Update
contentHtml:
Hey Brew Shop Fam!
Here's an update of what's happening this week - from new beer drops, wine reviews, our current tap list, to upcoming tastings, and more. 

We're excited to bring back The Brew Shop 6 Roses of Spring! Nothing hails the beginning of warm weather quite like Rose and we're here to help you ring it in with 6 fresh Roses. Available in limited quantities by pre-order, make sure you grab yours before they sell out!

Our 6 Roses of Spring will be hand-selected by your favorite winos (Beth, Julie & Aidan) and filled with never-before-seen-in-the-shop Rose. What's included is a surprise, but each pack will contain ONE sparkler and FIVE still Roses.

6 Roses of Spring packs will be available for pick-up beginning Thursday, May 7th - just in time for Mother's Day, if that special Mom in your life adores Rose!Wine Club Member? Remember to put in your special code at check out for 10% off!

Join us this Friday from 5-7 PM as we welcome Chris from Metro Cellars for our free tasting! Scroll down to see our full tasting lineup for April!

👻 Parish is back!
• Ghost in the Machine DIPA with Citra
• Actias Prime TIPA with Strata & Strata CGX Nuvo
• Bloom IPA with Citra & Nugget

🔭 Spyglass back in action
• Il Centro Italian Pilsner
• South Island New Zealand Pilsner with Nectaron & NZ Cascade
• Axion Hazy IPA with Citra Incognito, Citra, Rakau & Motueka
• Decoherence DIPA with Citra, Motueka, Nelson & Amarillo
• Object Oriented Hazy DIPA with Citra, Mosaic, Rakau & Simcoe
• Foundation Model TDH Hazy DIPA with Citra, Rakau & Nelson
• Fuzzier Logic Hazy DIPA with Citra, Nelson Sauvin & Galaxy

🎈 DC Fav Lost Generation
• Meridian of Time West Coast Pilsner with Nelson & Vista
• Cooling Twilight Vienna Lager
• Fearless and Free Hazy DIPA with Citra & Riwaka

🌅 Sunshine ready
• Schlafly Raspberry Hefe
• Great Lakes Sunshine Party Pack with Mexican Lager With Lime, Rally Drum Lemon Shandy, Orange & Honey Wheat, and Orange & Lime Shandy
• Destihl Hawaii Five Ale Tropical Blonde with pineapple, coconut, mango, peach & guava
• Athletic Fruited Fields Non-Alcoholic Radler

🦪 From the incomparable Josh Chapman
• Upweller Salts Tart Wheat Beer with Oysters
• Sojourn Celle Bock - collab with Benchtop
• Sojourn Before Sunset English Pale Ale - collab with Bluejacket

🗞️ Local, local!
• Vasen Mexican Lager
• Vasen North Slope New Zealand IPA
• Virginia Beer Co Trail Magic Brown Ale

🤲 Hops for the whole family
• Firestone Walker Pfreal West Coast IPA with (collab with Pfriem)
• Maine Peeper Pale Ale with Cascade, Centennial & Magnum
• Lagunitas Hop Stoopid West Coast DIPA

🐈 Alternate Ending got claws
• Amity Beer Lager
• Pivovarsky Bal Czech Foeder Lager
• Cottonmouth Hazy Session IPA
• Purrpertrators Hazy IPA with Pacific Sunrise, Mosaic, El Dorado & Nelson Hop Juice
• Stop Worrying and Have a Beer Czech Amber Lager

🌺 Tropical Vacay sours
• Evil Twin Make it Fruity Spring Has Sprung Sour with Mango, Passionfruit & Guava
• Feel the Rain Brothers Gosecolada Pineapple Coconut Gose
• Commonwealth Conch Republic Gose with Pink Guava, Lime, Papaya & Passion Fruit

💪🏻 Small but mighty
• Morning Recovery Sugar Free Watermelon is here

Final Gravity Supernova Hazy IPA

Benchtop Stanley’s Pilsner

Grimm Chance Encounter West Coast IPA

Pomalo Wine Co Debit: Pomalo means, roughly, relax / take it easy in Croatian, and this adorable bottle is absolutely giving that vibe. Take a vacation in your mind by popping open this refreshing and crushable white. Citrus, pear, and a hint of green apple plus a refreshing brininess and crisp acidity. Practicing organic, hand harvested, native yeast.

Domaine de la Tonnellerie Sancerre: This Sancerre had us at first sip! We're heading to a 13-hectare estate located in the Bue area of Sancerre where Domaine de la Tonnellerie has perfected the balance between mineral finesse, soft fruit and snappy citrus.

Mas de Boislauzon La Chaussynette: Siblings Christine and Daniel Chaussy (get it, Chaussynette?!) are the fifth generation to work their family’s domaine in Rhône with vineyards classified all the way up to the prestigious Chateauneuf-du-Pape appellation. Take a note from the label and share this playful bottle with friends and family. Fresh raspberries and cherries blend with rustic undertones of floral and pepper. Hand harvested, native yeast.

Roots Wine Company Klee Pinot Noir: After graduating from college, Chris followed his parents from the midwest to Willamette where he planted a small vineyard. The name "Roots" is a tribute to his hometown of Racine, Wisconsin which translates to roots in French. Klee (pronounced "clay") Pinot Noir is a steal for the price with dark cherry and brambly raspberry accompanied by baking spice and dark chocolate. Organic, Practicing Biodynamic, Native Yeast, Unfined, Vegan.

Three years ago we included Avantis Estate Amygdalies Rose in our 6 Roses of Spring release. It became my warm-weather obsession and I secretly hoarded bottles just for myself. Yes, that factually does happen often - just ask Julie. As we've been shifting our wine selection for the impending warm days, it occurs to me that you all have also been crushing on Greek wines, specifically the Avantis Estate Historia and Mitravelas Red on Black. Me too, so I've decided to play fair and let everyone else try the infamous Amygdalies Rose.

Avantis has been quietly doing excellent work on the sun-drenched slopes of Evia, Greece for years. Made with Syrah, Amygdalies rests on the grape skins for one day to extract the most gorgeous pale salmon-pink hue. The nose is fresh and fruity  - think strawberries, watermelon and a little squeeze of citrus. There's lovely red fruit through the mid-palate (hello, cherry and raspberry) and just enough heft to withstand your perfectly curated Mezze platter. Amygdalies means "almond" in Greek and, sure enough, there's a delicate nuttiness woven through the whole experience that makes it feel a little more complex than your average patio pour.

If you've yet to experience the joy of Greek wine stateside or otherwise, Amygdalies is a perfect gateway into why Greek wine deserves a permanent spot in your rotation. And, as added bonus points, Amygdalies hits the wallet nicely at $14.99 with an outdoor friendly screwcap.

This week’s writeup is brought to you by Beth.

April Tasting Events are spring ready!

Thanks for your business! See you soon, Beth, Julie & Aidan and the entire Brew Crew
